dialogue1.phrase: Time to face a real Contract!

dialogue2.phrase: Contracts have three main areas. I'll show you quickly before someone asks for an impossible deadline.

dialogue3.phrase: This left area contains the full contract description. Here you'll find the project context, client information, and important details about the required system.

dialogue4.phrase: Sometimes the client explains everything perfectly. Sometimes they write three pages without saying absolutely anything.

dialogue5.phrase: From my experience, they have one thing in common: they don't like building a system from scratch. They prefer you modify the existing architecture base of their system.

dialogue6.phrase: ...Even if their choice is horrible for the purpose they want. We must respect it, they are the ones paying, after all…

dialogue7.phrase: This sticky note on the right is the summary description I made for you. It highlights the main objectives and the most important quality attributes for the contract.

dialogue8.phrase: I generated it with an LLM. If it's wrong, it's the model's hallucination, not mine.

dialogue9.phrase: And the central region is the Project Area! That's where you assemble your solution by choosing a software architecture pattern and team developers.

dialogue10.phrase: Just drag the chosen option from the panel that opens when you click on the respective boxes. To remove the choice, simply right‑click on them.

dialogue11.phrase:
Each architecture strengthens certain quality attributes.
Meanwhile, your developers contribute their own attributes, which will be applied to the chosen architectural pattern.

dialogue12.phrase:
A good team can compensate for weaknesses…
But not even a thousand developers can save a horrible architectural decision.

dialogue13.phrase: Well… maybe a thousand could. But HR wouldn't allow it.

dialogue14.phrase: Your goal is to combine everything correctly to satisfy the contract's requirements.

dialogue15.phrase: If you can't… well, at least we'll have a great post‑mortem report.
